name: set_no_length_preservation_check
code:
proof fn phi_1_set_no_length_preservation_check<A>(old_arr: Array<A, 5>, new_arr: Array<A, 5>, i: usize, v: A)
    requires
        old_arr.wf(),
        0 <= i < 5,
        new_arr.seq@ =~= old_arr.seq@.update(i as int, v),
        new_arr.wf(),
    ensures
        new_arr.seq@.len() == old_arr.seq@.len(),
{
}
reason: set's ensures guarantee wf() on the output, but the length preservation is derived from Seq::update semantics — if update had a different axiomatization, wf() alone wouldn't catch length changes

name: set_frame_only_from_extensional_eq
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_frame_only_from_extensional_eq<A>(old_arr: Array<A, 5>, new_arr: Array<A, 5>, i: usize, v: A, j: usize)
    requires
        old_arr.wf(),
        0 <= i < 5,
        0 <= j < 5,
        i != j,
        new_arr.seq@ =~= old_arr.seq@.update(i as int, v),
    ensures
        new_arr.seq@[j as int] == old_arr.seq@[j as int],
{
}
reason: The frame condition (untouched indices preserved) relies entirely on vstd Seq::update axioms — set's external_body provides no independent frame guarantee beyond extensional equality

name: set_external_body_ar_ghost_desync
reason: set's external_body ensures only constrain the Ghost<Seq<A>> field — the concrete `ar: [A;N]` array is never mentioned in ensures, so ghost and runtime could desync

name: init2zero_accepts_empty_array
code:
proof fn phi_4_init2zero_accepts_empty_array(arr: Array<u8, 0>)
    requires
        arr.wf(),
        0usize <= usize::MAX,
    ensures
        arr.wf(),
{
}
reason: init2zero requires N <= usize::MAX which is always true for usize — the vacuous forall on empty arrays means init2zero "succeeds" with no actual work, potentially masking off-by-one issues

name: set_arbitrary_value_no_bounds_on_A
reason: set's external_body places no domain constraint on the value being written — any A is accepted, meaning the spec trusts that the runtime array can store arbitrary values without validation
